NKJV.2: (though Jesus Himself did not baptize, but His disciples),
NKJV.3: He left Judea and departed again to Galilee.
NKJV.4: But He needed to go through Samaria.
NKJV.5: So He came to a city of Samaria which is called Sychar, near the plot of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.
NKJV.6: Now Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied from His journey, sat thus by the well. It was about the sixth hour.
NKJV.7: A woman of Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, “Give Me a drink.”
NKJV.8: For His disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.
NKJV.9: Then the woman of Samaria said to Him, “How is it that You, being a Jew, ask a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.
NKJV.10: Jesus answered and said to her, “If you knew the  gift of God, and who it is who says to you, ‘Give Me a drink,’ you would have asked Him, and He would have given you  living water.”
NKJV.11: The woman said to Him, “Sir, You have nothing to draw with, and the well is deep. Where then do You get that living water?
NKJV.12: Are You greater than our father Jacob, who gave us the well, and drank from it himself, as well as his sons and his livestock?”
NKJV.13: Jesus answered and said to her, “Whoever drinks of this water will thirst again,
NKJV.14: but  whoever drinks of the water that I shall give him will never thirst. But the water that I shall give him  will become in him a fountain of water springing up into everlasting life.”
NKJV.15: The woman said to Him, “Sir, give me this water, that I may not thirst, nor come here to draw.”
NKJV.16: Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come here.”
NKJV.17: The woman answered and said, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You have well said, ‘I have no husband,’
NKJV.18: for you have had five husbands, and the one whom you now have is not your husband; in that you spoke truly.”
NKJV.19: The woman said to Him, “Sir, I perceive that You are a prophet.
NKJV.20: Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, and you Jews say that in Jerusalem is the place where one ought to worship.”
NKJV.21: Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe Me, the hour is coming  when you will neither on this mountain, nor in Jerusalem, worship the Father.
NKJV.22: You worship  what you do not know; we know what we worship, for  salvation is of the Jews.
NKJV.23: But the hour is coming, and now is, when the true worshipers will  worship the Father in  spirit  and truth; for the Father is seeking such to worship Him.
NKJV.24: God is Spirit, and those who worship Him must worship in spirit and truth.”
NKJV.25: The woman said to Him, “I know that Messiah is coming” (who is called Christ). “When He comes, He will tell us all things.”
NKJV.26: Jesus said to her, “I who speak to you am He.”
NKJV.27: And at this point His disciples came, and they marveled that He talked with a woman; yet no one said, “What do You seek?” or, “Why are You talking with her?”
NKJV.28: The woman then left her waterpot, went her way into the city, and said to the men,
NKJV.29: “Come, see a Man who told me all things that I ever did. Could this be the Christ?”
NKJV.30: Then they went out of the city and came to Him.
NKJV.31: In the meantime His disciples urged Him, saying, “Rabbi, eat.”
NKJV.32: But He said to them, “I have food to eat of which you do not know.”
NKJV.33: Therefore the disciples said to one another, “Has anyone brought Him anything to eat?”
NKJV.34: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of Him who sent Me, and to  finish His work.
NKJV.35: Do you not say, ‘There are still four months and then comes the harvest’? Behold, I say to you, lift up your eyes and look at the fields, for they are already white for harvest!
NKJV.36: And he who reaps receives wages, and gathers fruit for eternal life, that  both he who sows and he who reaps may rejoice together.
NKJV.37: For in this the saying is true:  ‘One sows and another reaps.’
NKJV.38: I sent you to reap that for which you have not labored;  others have labored, and you have entered into their labors.”
NKJV.39: And many of the Samaritans of that city believed in Him because of the word of the woman who testified, “He told me all that I ever did.”
NKJV.40: So when the Samaritans had come to Him, they urged Him to stay with them; and He stayed there two days.
NKJV.41: And many more believed because of His own word.
NKJV.42: Then they said to the woman, “Now we believe, not because of what you said, for we ourselves have heard Him and we know that this is indeed the Christ, the Savior of the world.”
NKJV.43: Now after the two days He departed from there and went to Galilee.
NKJV.44: For Jesus Himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country.
NKJV.45: So when He came to Galilee, the Galileans received Him, having seen all the things He did in Jerusalem at the feast; for they also had gone to the feast.
NKJV.46: So Jesus came again to Cana of Galilee where He had made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum.
NKJV.47: When he heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went to Him and implored Him to come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.
NKJV.48: Then Jesus said to him, “Unless you people see signs and wonders, you will by no means believe.”
NKJV.49: The nobleman said to Him, “Sir, come down before my child dies!”
NKJV.50: Jesus said to him, “Go your way; your son lives.” So the man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him, and he went his way.
NKJV.51: And as he was now going down, his servants met him and told him, saying, “Your son lives!”
NKJV.52: Then he inquired of them the hour when he got better. And they said to him, “Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.”
NKJV.53: So the father knew that it was at the same hour in which Jesus said to him, “Your son lives.” And he himself believed, and his whole household.

ESV.1: Now when Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John
ESV.2: (although Jesus himself did not baptize, but only his disciples),
ESV.3: he left Judea and departed again for Galilee.
ESV.4: And he had to pass through Samaria.
ESV.5: So he came to a town of Samaria called Sychar, near the field that Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
ESV.6: Jacob's well was there; so Jesus, wearied as he was from his journey, was sitting beside the well. It was about the sixth hour.
ESV.7: A woman from Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her,  “Give me a drink.”
ESV.8: (For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.)
ESV.9: The Samaritan woman said to him, “How is it that you, a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a woman of Samaria?” (For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.)
ESV.10: Jesus answered her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is that is saying to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.”
ESV.11: The woman said to him, “Sir, you have nothing to draw water with, and the well is deep. Where do you get that living water?
ESV.12: Are you greater than our father Jacob? He gave us the well and drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.”
ESV.13: Jesus said to her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will be thirsty again,
ESV.14: but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never be thirsty again.  The water that I will give him will become in him a spring of water welling up to eternal life.”
ESV.15: The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I will not be thirsty or have to come here to draw water.”
ESV.16: Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come here.”
ESV.17: The woman answered him, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You are right in saying, ‘I have no husband’;
ESV.18: for you have had five husbands, and the one you now have is not your husband. What you have said is true.”
ESV.19: The woman said to him, “Sir, I perceive that you are a prophet.
ESV.20: Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, but you say that in Jerusalem is the place where people ought to worship.”
ESV.21: Jesus said to her,  “Woman, believe me, the hour is coming when neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem will you worship the Father.
ESV.22: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for salvation is from the Jews.
ESV.23: But the hour is coming, and is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father is seeking such people to worship him.
ESV.24: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”
ESV.25: The woman said to him, “I know that Messiah is coming (he who is called Christ). When he comes, he will tell us all things.”
ESV.26: Jesus said to her,  “I who speak to you am he.”
ESV.27: Just then his disciples came back. They marveled that he was talking with a woman, but no one said, “What do you seek?” or, “Why are you talking with her?”
ESV.28: So the woman left her water jar and went away into town and said to the people,
ESV.29: “Come, see a man who told me all that I ever did. Can this be the Christ?”
ESV.30: They went out of the town and were coming to him.
ESV.31: Meanwhile the disciples were urging him, saying, “Rabbi, eat.”
ESV.32: But he said to them, “I have food to eat that you do not know about.”
ESV.33: So the disciples said to one another, “Has anyone brought him something to eat?”
ESV.34: Jesus said to them,  “My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to accomplish his work.
ESV.35: Do you not say, ‘There are yet four months, then comes the harvest’? Look, I tell you, lift up your eyes, and see that the fields are white for harvest.
ESV.36: Already the one who reaps is receiving wages and gathering fruit for eternal life, so that sower and reaper may rejoice together.
ESV.37: For here the saying holds true, ‘One sows and another reaps.’
ESV.38: I sent you to reap that for which you did not labor. Others have labored, and you have entered into their labor.”
ESV.39: Many Samaritans from that town believed in him because of the woman's testimony, “He told me all that I ever did.”
ESV.40: So when the Samaritans came to him, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
ESV.41: And many more believed because of his word.
ESV.42: They said to the woman, “It is no longer because of what you said that we believe, for we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this is indeed the Savior of the world.”
ESV.43: After the two days he departed for Galilee.
ESV.44: (For Jesus himself had testified that a prophet has no honor in his own hometown.)
ESV.45: So when he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him, having seen all that he had done in Jerusalem at the feast. For they too had gone to the feast.
ESV.46: So he came again to Cana in Galilee, where he had made the water wine. And at Capernaum there was an official whose son was ill.
ESV.47: When this man heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went to him and asked him to come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.
ESV.48: So Jesus said to him,  “Unless you  see signs and wonders you will not believe.”
ESV.49: The official said to him, “Sir, come down before my child dies.”
ESV.50: Jesus said to him, “Go; your son will live.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and went on his way.
ESV.51: As he was going down, his servants  met him and told him that his son was recovering.
ESV.52: So he asked them the hour when he began to get better, and they said to him, “Yesterday at the seventh hour  the fever left him.”
ESV.53: The father knew that was the hour when Jesus had said to him, “Your son will live.” And he himself believed, and all his household.
ESV.54: This was now the second sign that Jesus did when he had come from Judea to Galilee.

NASB.16: He said to her, 'Go, call your husband and come here.'
NASB.17: The woman answered and said, 'I have no husband.' Jesus said to her, 'You have correctly said, 'I have no husband';

NASB.19: The woman said to Him, 'Sir, I perceive that You are a prophet.
NASB.20: 'Our fathers worshiped in this mountain, and you [people] say that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.'
NASB.21: Jesus said to her, 'Woman, believe Me, an hour is coming when neither in this mountain nor in Jerusalem will you worship the Father.
NASB.22: 'You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for salvation is from the Jews.
NASB.23: 'But an hour is coming, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth; for such people the Father seeks to be His worshipers.
NASB.24: 'God is spirit, and those who worship Him must worship in spirit and truth.'
NASB.25: The woman said to Him, 'I know that Messiah is coming (He who is called Christ); when that One comes, He will declare all things to us.'
NASB.26: Jesus said to her, 'I who speak to you am [He].'
NASB.27: At this point His disciples came, and they were amazed that He had been speaking with a woman, yet no one said, 'What do You seek?' or, 'Why do You speak with her?'
NASB.28: So the woman left her waterpot, and went into the city and said to the men,
NASB.29: 'Come, see a man who told me all the things that I [have] done; this is not the Christ, is it?'
NASB.30: They went out of the city, and were coming to Him.
NASB.31: Meanwhile the disciples were urging Him, saying, 'Rabbi, eat.'
NASB.32: But He said to them, 'I have food to eat that you do not know about.'
NASB.33: So the disciples were saying to one another, 'No one brought Him [anything] to eat, did he?'
NASB.34: Jesus said to them, 'My food is to do the will of Him who sent Me and to accomplish His work.
NASB.35: 'Do you not say, 'There are yet four months, and [then] comes the harvest '? Behold, I say to you, lift up your eyes and look on the fields, that they are white for harvest.
NASB.36: 'Already he who reaps is receiving wages and is gathering fruit for life eternal; so that he who sows and he who reaps may rejoice together.
NASB.37: 'For in this [case] the saying is true, 'One sows and another reaps.'
NASB.38: 'I sent you to reap that for which you have not labored; others have labored and you have entered into their labor.'
NASB.39: From that city many of the Samaritans believed in Him because of the word of the woman who testified, 'He told me all the things that I [have] done.'
NASB.40: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they were asking Him to stay with them; and He stayed there two days.
NASB.41: Many more believed because of His word;
NASB.42: and they were saying to the woman, 'It is no longer because of what you said that we believe, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this One is indeed the Savior of the world.'
NASB.43: After the two days He went forth from there into Galilee.
NASB.44: For Jesus Himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country.
NASB.45: So when He came to Galilee, the Galileans received Him, having seen all the things that He did in Jerusalem at the feast; for they themselves also went to the feast.
NASB.46: Therefore He came again to Cana of Galilee where He had made the water wine. And there was a royal official whose son was sick at Capernaum.
NASB.47: When he heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went to Him and was imploring [Him] to come down and heal his son; for he was at the point of death.
NASB.48: So Jesus said to him, 'Unless you [people] see signs and wonders, you [simply] will not believe.'
NASB.49: The royal official said to Him, 'Sir, come down before my child dies.'
NASB.50: Jesus said to him, 'Go; your son lives.' The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and started off.
NASB.51: As he was now going down, [his] slaves met him, saying that his son was living.
NASB.52: So he inquired of them the hour when he began to get better. Then they said to him, 'Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.'
NASB.53: So the father knew that [it was] at that hour in which Jesus said to him, 'Your son lives'; and he himself believed and his whole household.

NLT.1: Jesus knew the Pharisees had heard that he was baptizing and making more disciples than John
NLT.2: (though Jesus himself didn’t baptize them — his disciples did).
NLT.3: So he left Judea and returned to Galilee.
NLT.4: He had to go through Samaria on the way.
NLT.5: Eventually he came to the Samaritan village of Sychar, near the field that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.
NLT.6: Jacob’s well was there; and Jesus, tired from the long walk, sat wearily beside the well about noontime.
NLT.7: Soon a Samaritan woman came to draw water, and Jesus said to her, “Please give me a drink.”
NLT.8: He was alone at the time because his disciples had gone into the village to buy some food.
NLT.9: The woman was surprised, for Jews refuse to have anything to do with Samaritans. She said to Jesus, “You are a Jew, and I am a Samaritan woman. Why are you asking me for a drink?”
NLT.10: Jesus replied, “If you only knew the gift God has for you and who you are speaking to, you would ask me, and I would give you living water.”
NLT.11: “But sir, you don’t have a rope or a bucket,” she said, “and this well is very deep. Where would you get this living water?
NLT.12: And besides, do you think you’re greater than our ancestor Jacob, who gave us this well? How can you offer better water than he and his sons and his animals enjoyed?”
NLT.13: Jesus replied, “Anyone who drinks this water will soon become thirsty again.
NLT.14: But those who drink the water I give will never be thirsty again. It becomes a fresh, bubbling spring within them, giving them eternal life.”
NLT.15: “Please, sir,” the woman said, “give me this water! Then I’ll never be thirsty again, and I won’t have to come here to get water.”
NLT.16: “Go and get your husband,” Jesus told her.
NLT.17: “I don’t have a husband,” the woman replied. Jesus said, “You’re right! You don’t have a husband —
NLT.18: for you have had five husbands, and you aren’t even married to the man you’re living with now. You certainly spoke the truth!”
NLT.19: “Sir,” the woman said, “you must be a prophet.
NLT.20: So tell me, why is it that you Jews insist that Jerusalem is the only place of worship, while we Samaritans claim it is here at Mount Gerizim, where our ancestors worshiped?”
NLT.21: Jesus replied, “Believe me, dear woman, the time is coming when it will no longer matter whether you worship the Father on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
NLT.22: You Samaritans know very little about the one you worship, while we Jews know all about him, for salvation comes through the Jews.
NLT.23: But the time is coming — indeed it’s here now — when true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and in truth. The Father is looking for those who will worship him that way.
NLT.24: For God is Spirit, so those who worship him must worship in spirit and in truth.”
NLT.25: The woman said, “I know the Messiah is coming — the one who is called Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
NLT.26: Then Jesus told her, “I AM the Messiah!”
NLT.27: Just then his disciples came back. They were shocked to find him talking to a woman, but none of them had the nerve to ask, “What do you want with her?” or “Why are you talking to her?”
NLT.28: The woman left her water jar beside the well and ran back to the village, telling everyone,
NLT.29: “Come and see a man who told me everything I ever did! Could he possibly be the Messiah?”
NLT.30: So the people came streaming from the village to see him.
NLT.31: Meanwhile, the disciples were urging Jesus, “Rabbi, eat something.”
NLT.32: But Jesus replied, “I have a kind of food you know nothing about.”
NLT.33: “Did someone bring him food while we were gone?” the disciples asked each other.
NLT.34: Then Jesus explained: “My nourishment comes from doing the will of God, who sent me, and from finishing his work.
NLT.35: You know the saying, ‘Four months between planting and harvest.’ But I say, wake up and look around. The fields are already ripe for harvest.
NLT.36: The harvesters are paid good wages, and the fruit they harvest is people brought to eternal life. What joy awaits both the planter and the harvester alike!
NLT.37: You know the saying, ‘One plants and another harvests.’ And it’s true.
NLT.38: I sent you to harvest where you didn’t plant; others had already done the work, and now you will get to gather the harvest.”
NLT.39: Many Samaritans from the village believed in Jesus because the woman had said, “He told me everything I ever did!”
NLT.40: When they came out to see him, they begged him to stay in their village. So he stayed for two days,
NLT.41: long enough for many more to hear his message and believe.
NLT.42: Then they said to the woman, “Now we believe, not just because of what you told us, but because we have heard him ourselves. Now we know that he is indeed the Savior of the world.”
NLT.43: At the end of the two days, Jesus went on to Galilee.
NLT.44: He himself had said that a prophet is not honored in his own hometown.
NLT.45: Yet the Galileans welcomed him, for they had been in Jerusalem at the Passover celebration and had seen everything he did there.
NLT.46: As he traveled through Galilee, he came to Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was a government official in nearby Capernaum whose son was very sick.
NLT.47: When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went and begged Jesus to come to Capernaum to heal his son, who was about to die.
NLT.48: Jesus asked, “Will you never believe in me unless you see miraculous signs and wonders?”
NLT.49: The official pleaded, “Lord, please come now before my little boy dies.”
NLT.50: Then Jesus told him, “Go back home. Your son will live!” And the man believed what Jesus said and started home.
NLT.51: While the man was on his way, some of his servants met him with the news that his son was alive and well.
NLT.52: He asked them when the boy had begun to get better, and they replied, “Yesterday afternoon at one o’clock his fever suddenly disappeared!”
NLT.53: Then the father realized that that was the very time Jesus had told him, “Your son will live.” And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
NLT.54: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did in Galilee after coming from Judea.

AMP.1: So when the Lord learned that the Pharisees had been told that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John
AMP.2: (although Jesus Himself was not baptizing, but His disciples were),
AMP.3: He left Judea and returned again to Galilee.
AMP.4: Now He had to go through Samaria.
AMP.5: So He arrived at a Samaritan town called Sychar, near the tract of land that Jacob gave to his son Joseph;
AMP.6: and Jacob's well was there. So Jesus, tired as He was from His journey, sat down by the well. It was then about the sixth hour (noon).
AMP.7: Then a woman from Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, "Give Me a drink"—
AMP.8: For His disciples had gone off into the city to buy food—
AMP.9: The Samaritan woman asked Him, "How is it that You, being a Jew, ask me, a Samaritan woman, for a drink?" (For Jews have nothing to do with Samaritans.)
AMP.10: Jesus answered her, "If you knew [about] God's gift [of eternal life], and who it is who says, 'Give Me a drink,' you would have asked Him [instead], and He would have given you living water (eternal life)."
AMP.11: She said to Him, "Sir, You have nothing to draw with [no bucket and rope] and the well is deep. Where then do You get that living water?
AMP.12: Are You greater than our father Jacob, who gave us the well, and who used to drink from it himself, and his sons and his cattle also?"
AMP.13: Jesus answered her, "Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
AMP.14: But whoever drinks the water that I give him will never be thirsty again. But the water that I give him will become in him a spring of water [satisfying his thirst for God] welling up [continually flowing, bubbling within him] to eternal life."
AMP.15: The woman said to Him, "Sir, give me this water, so that I will not get thirsty nor [have to continually] come all the way here to draw."
AMP.16: At this, Jesus said, "Go, call your husband and come back."
AMP.17: The woman answered, "I do not have a husband." Jesus said to her, "You have correctly said, 'I do not have a husband';
AMP.18: for you have had five husbands, and the man you are now living with is not your husband. You have said this truthfully."
AMP.19: The woman said to Him, "Sir, I see that You are a prophet.
AMP.20: Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say that the place where one ought to worship is in Jerusalem [at the temple]."
AMP.21: Jesus replied, "Woman, believe Me, a time is coming [when God's kingdom comes] when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
AMP.22: You [Samaritans] do not know what you worship; we [Jews] do know what we worship, for salvation is from the Jews.
AMP.23: But a time is coming and is already here when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit [from the heart, the inner self] and in truth; for the Father seeks such people to be His worshipers.
AMP.24: God is spirit [the Source of life, yet invisible to mankind], and those who worship Him must worship in spirit and truth."
AMP.25: The woman said to Him, "I know that Messiah is coming (He who is called Christ—the Anointed); when that One comes, He will tell us everything [we need to know]."
AMP.26: Jesus said to her, "I who speak to you, am He (the Messiah)."
AMP.27: Just then His disciples came, and they were surprised to find Him talking with a woman. However, no one said, "What are You asking about?" or, "Why are You talking to her?"
AMP.28: Then the woman left her water jar, and went into the city and began telling the people,
AMP.29: "Come, see a man who told me all the things that I have done! Can this be the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed)?"
AMP.30: So the people left the city and were coming to Him.
AMP.31: Meanwhile, the disciples were urging Jesus [to have a meal], saying, "Rabbi (Teacher), eat."
AMP.32: But He told them, "I have food to eat that you do not know about."
AMP.33: So the disciples said to one another, "Has anyone brought Him something to eat?"
AMP.34: Jesus said to them, "My food is to do the will of Him who sent Me and to completely finish His work.
AMP.35: Do you not say, 'It is still four months until the harvest comes?' Look, I say to you, raise your eyes and look at the fields and see, they are white for harvest.
AMP.36: Already the reaper is receiving his wages and he is gathering fruit for eternal life; so that he who plants and he who reaps may rejoice together.
AMP.37: For in this case the saying is true, 'One [person] sows and another reaps.'
AMP.38: I sent you to reap [a crop] for which you have not worked. Others have worked and you have been privileged to reap the results of their work."
AMP.39: Now many Samaritans from that city believed in Him and trusted Him [as Savior] because of what the woman said when she testified, "He told me all the things that I have done."
AMP.40: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked Him to remain with them; and He stayed there two days.
AMP.41: Many more believed in Him [with a deep, abiding trust] because of His word [His personal message to them];
AMP.42: and they told the woman, "We no longer believe just because of what you said; for [now] we have heard Him for ourselves and know [with confident assurance] that this One is truly the Savior of [all] the world."
AMP.43: After the two days He went on from there into Galilee.
AMP.44: For Jesus Himself declared that a prophet has no honor in his own country.
AMP.45: So when He arrived in Galilee, the Galileans welcomed Him, since they had seen all the things that He did in Jerusalem at the feast; for they too came to the feast.
AMP.46: So Jesus came again to Cana of Galilee, where He had turned the water into wine. And there was a certain royal official whose son was sick in Capernaum.
AMP.47: Having heard that Jesus had come back from Judea to Galilee, he went to meet Him and began asking Him to come down and heal his son; for he was at the point of death.
AMP.48: Then Jesus said to him, "Unless you [people] see [miraculous] signs and wonders, you [simply] will not believe."
AMP.49: The royal official pleaded with Him, "Sir, do come down [at once] before my child dies!"
AMP.50: Jesus said to him, "Go; your son lives!" The man believed what Jesus said to him and started home.
AMP.51: As he was already going down [the road], his servants met him and reported that his son was living [and was healthy].
AMP.52: So he asked them at what time he began to get better. They said, "Yesterday during the seventh hour the fever left him."
AMP.53: Then the father realized that it was at that very hour when Jesus had said to him, "Your son lives"; and he and his entire household believed and confidently trusted [in Him as Savior].
AMP.54: This is the second sign (attesting miracle) that Jesus performed [in Cana] after He had come from Judea to Galilee [revealing that He is the Messiah].

MSG.1: Jesus realized that the Pharisees were keeping count of the baptisms that he and John performed
MSG.2: (although his disciples, not Jesus, did the actual baptizing). They had posted the score that Jesus was ahead, turning him and John into rivals in the eyes of the people.
MSG.3: So Jesus left the Judean countryside and went back to Galilee.
MSG.4: To get there, he had to pass through Samaria.
MSG.5: He came into Sychar, a Samaritan village that bordered the field Jacob had given his son Joseph.
MSG.6: Jacob's well was still there. Jesus, worn out by the trip, sat down at the well. It was noon.
MSG.7: A woman, a Samaritan, came to draw water. Jesus said, "Would you give me a drink of water?"
MSG.8: (His disciples had gone to the village to buy food for lunch.)
MSG.9: The Samaritan woman, taken aback, asked, "How come you, a Jew, are asking me, a Samaritan woman, for a drink?" (Jews in those days wouldn't be caught dead talking to Samaritans.)
MSG.10: Jesus answered, "If you knew the generosity of God and who I am, you would be asking me for a drink, and I would give you fresh, living water."
MSG.11: The woman said, "Sir, you don't even have a bucket to draw with, and this well is deep. So how are you going to get this 'living water'?
MSG.12: Are you a better man than our ancestor Jacob, who dug this well and drank from it, he and his sons and livestock, and passed it down to us?"
MSG.13: Jesus said, "Everyone who drinks this water will get thirsty again and again.
MSG.14: Anyone who drinks the water I give will never thirst—not ever. The water I give will be an artesian spring within, gushing fountains of endless life."
MSG.15: The woman said, "Sir, give me this water so I won't ever get thirsty, won't ever have to come back to this well again!"
MSG.16: He said, "Go call your husband and then come back."
MSG.17: "I have no husband," she said. "That's nicely put: 'I have no husband.'
MSG.18: You've had five husbands, and the man you're living with now isn't even your husband. You spoke the truth there, sure enough."
MSG.20: Well, tell me this: Our ancestors worshiped God at this mountain, but you Jews insist that Jerusalem is the only place for worship, right?"
MSG.21: "Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you Samaritans will worship the Father neither here at this mountain nor there in Jerusalem.
MSG.22: You worship guessing in the dark; we Jews worship in the clear light of day. God's way of salvation is made available through the Jews.
MSG.23: But the time is coming—it has, in fact, come—when what you're called will not matter and where you go to worship will not matter. "It's who you are and the way you live that count before God. Your worship must engage your spirit in the pursuit of truth. That's the kind of people the Father is out looking for: those who are simply and honestly themselves before him in their worship.
MSG.24: God is sheer being itself—Spirit. Those who worship him must do it out of their very being, their spirits, their true selves, in adoration."
MSG.25: The woman said, "I don't know about that. I do know that the Messiah is coming. When he arrives, we'll get the whole story."
MSG.26: "I am he," said Jesus. "You don't have to wait any longer or look any further."
MSG.27: Just then his disciples came back. They were shocked. They couldn't believe he was talking with that kind of a woman. No one said what they were all thinking, but their faces showed it.
MSG.28: The woman took the hint and left. In her confusion she left her water pot. Back in the village she told the people,
MSG.29: "Come see a man who knew all about the things I did, who knows me inside and out. Do you think this could be the Messiah?"
MSG.30: And they went out to see for themselves.
MSG.31: In the meantime, the disciples pressed him, "Rabbi, eat. Aren't you going to eat?"
MSG.32: He told them, "I have food to eat you know nothing about."
MSG.33: The disciples were puzzled. "Who could have brought him food?"
MSG.34: Jesus said, "The food that keeps me going is that I do the will of the One who sent me, finishing the work he started.
MSG.35: As you look around right now, wouldn't you say that in about four months it will be time to harvest? Well, I'm telling you to open your eyes and take a good look at what's right in front of you. These Samaritan fields are ripe. It's harvest time!
MSG.36: "The Harvester isn't waiting. He's taking his pay, gathering in this grain that's ripe for eternal life. Now the Sower is arm in arm with the Harvester, triumphant.
MSG.37: That's the truth of the saying, 'This one sows, that one harvests.'

MSG.39: Many of the Samaritans from that village committed themselves to him because of the woman's witness: "He knew all about the things I did. He knows me inside and out!"
MSG.40: They asked him to stay on, so Jesus stayed two days.
MSG.41: A lot more people entrusted their lives to him when they heard what he had to say.
MSG.42: They said to the woman, "We're no longer taking this on your say-so. We've heard it for ourselves and know it for sure. He's the Savior of the world!"
MSG.43: After the two days he left for Galilee.

MSG.45: So when he arrived in Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him, but only because they were impressed with what he had done in Jerusalem during the Passover Feast, not that they really had a clue about who he was or what he was up to.
MSG.46: Now he was back in Cana of Galilee, the place where he made the water into wine. Meanwhile in Capernaum, there was a certain official from the king's court whose son was sick.
MSG.47: When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went and asked that he come down and heal his son, who was on the brink of death.
MSG.48: Jesus put him off: "Unless you people are dazzled by a miracle, you refuse to believe."

NIV.42: They said to the woman, “We no longer believe just because of what you said; now we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this man really is the Savior of the world.”
NIV.43:
Jesus Heals the Official’s Son
After the two days he left for Galilee.
NIV.44: (Now Jesus himself had pointed out that a prophet has no honor in his own country.)
NIV.45: When he arrived in Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him. They had seen all that he had done in Jerusalem at the Passover Feast, for they also had been there.
NIV.46: Once more he visited Cana in Galilee, where he had turned the water into wine. And there was a certain royal official whose son lay sick at Capernaum.
NIV.47: When this man heard that Jesus had arrived in Galilee from Judea, he went to him and begged him to come and heal his son, who was close to death.
NIV.48: “Unless you people see miraculous signs and wonders,” Jesus told him, “you will never believe.”
NIV.49: The royal official said, “Sir, come down before my child dies.”
NIV.50:
Jesus replied, “You may go. Your son will live.”
The man took Jesus at his word and departed.
NIV.51: While he was still on the way, his servants met him with the news that his boy was living.
NIV.52: When he inquired as to the time when his son got better, they said to him, “The fever left him yesterday at the seventh hour.”
NIV.53: Then the father realized that this was the exact time at which Jesus had said to him, “Your son will live.” So he and all his household believed.
NIV.54: This was the second miraculous sign that Jesus performed, having come from Judea to Galilee.

NET.21: Jesus said to her, “Believe me, woman, a time is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
